raw_consumer_statement:
I signed up for service in XXXX annual {$750.00} because XXXX XXXX  was included. However the data was bad ( not current or not found at all compared to other services I use, that could find the owners info ) - I stopped using the service and get an email days before renewal, I requested cancelation the day due and was billed {$1400.00} XX/XX/XXXX. 
- I requested a refund and told ( no ) unless I can prove I requested the cancellation prior. 
- I call the toll free number and get an automated answer every time, left a message on four occasions and no return phone call. 
- I dispute the charge with the credit card and deal machine send them forged documents that I purchased the new subscription and signed up for service the month prior. 
( I saw forged because I did not sign up for service again I had not used the app or website for months, they also copied an IP address that is not mine to their documents ) ***oh it gets better they also tell AMEX that I cancelled on the XXXX of the month despite the fact I have an email confirming my request to cancel on the XXXX the same day I was charged. 

I now have a XXXX debt for a Software app that I have not used and will not use and all XXXX had to do was say ok we see you no longer want our services and have not used it we will reverse todays charge. 

American Express wants you to try to dispute with the merchant first so I attempted to and after no response from the merchant I filled a dispute with AMEX. 
The merchant sent AMEX there terms and conditions and stated there policy does not offer refunds and created documents that make it look as if I made a purchase when i did not. 
My error was not disputing it the same day however AMEX clearly requests that you try to resolve the issue with the merchant first. 
I stress this is not a purchase of goods or services. It is a subscription ( app access ) based service I did not purchase.
